name: Citation Intelligence & Source Authority
description: Audits citation quality across four dimensions: source authority (peer-reviewed vs. grey literature), citation style conformance (APA7/IEEE/UGC/Harvard), reference-list completeness vs. in-text citations, and multi-citation stacking without synthesis. Separate from plagiarism-check (PLG) and research-quality (QUA) — this skill focuses entirely on citation integrity and evidence authority.
ethical_boundary: Do not fabricate or invent any reference entry. Do not remove valid citations to lower similarity. Do not claim a DOI is invalid without checking it. Do not penalise legitimate grey literature when it is the primary source type for the field. Never replace a valid citation with a made-up one.

rules.description: Score what proportion of citations point to stable scholarly sources: peer-reviewed journal articles, conference papers with DOIs, published books, government/institutional reports. Penalise heavy reliance on blogs, Wikipedia, news articles, social media, or undated web pages.
rules.thresholds_note: 80%+ scholarly = Excellent, 60-79% = Adequate, 40-59% = Needs work, <40% = Critical

rules.description: Every in-text citation (Author, Year) or [N] must have a matching reference entry, and every reference entry must appear in-text at least once. Orphan in-text citations and orphan reference entries are both flagged.

Verify in-text and reference list format matches the selected norm: APA7 (Author, Year), IEEE [N], UGC thesis (Author, Year with footnote backup), or Harvard (Author Year). Mixed styles in the same document are flagged.

Flag sentences with 4 or more consecutive citations without synthesis or interpretation between them. Stacking signals low-quality literature engagement. Recommend grouping by theme with researcher commentary.

Verify DOIs follow the correct prefix pattern (10.XXXX/...). Flag malformed DOIs. Flag references missing DOIs where a DOI is likely available (journal articles, conference papers). Do NOT mark missing DOI as invalid for books or reports.

Flag URLs from unstable sources: social media, blog platforms (Medium, Substack, Blogger), Wikipedia, wikis without editorial control, news sites (unless citing as news events). Flag URLs without access dates for web sources.

Check proportion of citations published within the last 5 years vs. seminal works (>10 years old). A strong thesis mixes recent evidence with foundational theory. Target: 50%+ citations from the last 5 years for fast-moving fields (AI, cloud, cybersecurity). 30%+ for established fields (law, philosophy).
Detect whether the same author appears as the dominant citation source. More than 20% self-citation without acknowledged rationale may indicate confirmation bias or self-promotion.
Flag reference list entries that appear to be duplicates of the same source with minor formatting differences (same author surname, same year, similar title). Common after multiple editing rounds.

Score citation density by chapter. Literature Review should have highest density (2+ citations per claim paragraph). Methodology should cite the research design sources. Discussion should link findings back to cited literature. Conclusion with zero citations may be acceptable.
rules.thresholds_note: Lit Review: >60% cited sentences = Healthy. Methodology: >40%. Discussion: >30%.

rules.description: Detect use of 'as cited in' more than 5 times per chapter. Secondary citations should be avoided — the researcher should access the original source. Flag and recommend finding the primary source.

word_lists.replacement: find and cite the original source directly
cite the original peer-reviewed source instead
repeat the full citation — ibid causes confusion in digital documents

fix_reference_style.template:
Reformat these reference entries to comply with {norm} standard.

For APA7:
- Hanging indent, double-spaced
- Sentence case for article/chapter titles (capitalise first word and proper nouns only)
- Title case for journal names
- Include DOI as https://doi.org/... where available
- Alphabetical order by first author surname
- Year in parentheses after authors

For IEEE:
- Numbered list [1], [2], [3]...
- Author initials before surname
- Title in quotation marks for articles, italic for books
- Year at end of entry

For UGC:
- APA7 style with footnote number backup
- All entries must include place of publication for books

For Harvard:
- Author Surname, Initials. (Year) Title. Place: Publisher.

Do NOT invent DOIs or publication details.
Do NOT change author names or publication years.

References:
{text}

Target norm: {norm}

synthesise_stacked_citations.template:
This sentence has stacked citations without synthesis. Rewrite it as evidence-led commentary.

Original (stacked citations, no synthesis):
{text}

Rewrite rules:
1. Group the cited sources by the specific claim they support.
2. Add researcher interpretation connecting them.
3. Note any disagreement between sources.
4. Keep all citations — do not remove any.
5. Do not fabricate any source or data.

Document type: {doc_type}
Norm: {norm}

find_missing_references.template:
Audit this reference list against the in-text citations below.

In-text citations found:
{intext_citations}

Reference list entries:
{reference_list}

Output:
1. List every in-text citation with NO matching reference entry.
2. List every reference entry NOT cited in-text.
3. List any suspected duplicate reference entries.

Do NOT invent any reference entries. Flag gaps only.
